findings.title: Root account does not have MFA enabled
findings.description: The AWS root account has full, unrestricted access to the account. Without MFA, a leaked root password is a total account compromise.
findings.remediation: Enable a hardware or virtual MFA device on the root account immediately.

findings.title: S3 bucket is publicly accessible
findings.description: This bucket's ACL or bucket policy allows access from anyone on the internet.
findings.remediation: Enable S3 Block Public Access at the bucket (and account) level.

findings.title: Security group allows unrestricted inbound access on a sensitive port
findings.description: A security group permits inbound traffic from 0.0.0.0/0 on a sensitive port.
findings.remediation: Restrict the source CIDR to known IP ranges or remove the rule entirely.

findings.title: RDS instance is publicly accessible
findings.description: This database instance has a publicly resolvable endpoint reachable from the internet.

findings.title: IAM policy grants wildcard (Action:* / Resource:*) permissions
findings.description: A customer-managed policy allows all actions on all resources.
findings.remediation: Scope the policy down to specific actions and resource ARNs.

findings.title: No multi-region CloudTrail trail is enabled and logging
findings.description: Without an active multi-region trail, API activity in some regions goes unlogged.

findings.title: S3 bucket does not have default encryption enabled
findings.description: Objects written to this bucket are not encrypted at rest by default.

findings.title: RDS instance storage is not encrypted
findings.description: Data at rest on this RDS instance is not encrypted.
findings.remediation: Enable storage encryption via snapshot copy.

findings.title: EBS volume is not encrypted
findings.description: Data on this EBS volume is stored unencrypted at rest.

findings.title: EC2 instance has a public IP address
findings.description: This instance is directly reachable from the internet.
findings.remediation: Move behind a private subnet / load balancer / bastion if not needed.

findings.title: S3 bucket does not have access logging enabled
findings.description: Without access logging, requests cannot be audited after an incident.

findings.title: Default VPC is present and in use
findings.description: The default VPC has permissive default settings.
findings.remediation: Use custom VPCs with intentional subnetting/routing.
